hypotheses.description: Inspect target-direction displacement and test gait timing, traction authority, or measured slip recovery on this fixed case.
hypotheses.rationale: Survival alone did not produce the required signed target-direction progress or the robot moved backward beyond the locked allowance.

hypotheses.description: Inspect the frozen resting pose, recovery-target entry/exit events, and contact sequence; test a bounded pose-conditioned recovery state machine before changing the scenario.
hypotheses.rationale: The robot did not reach and hold the shared stable-standing target within the locked time envelope.

hypotheses.description: Compare rigid and articulated reachable recovery workspaces under the same controller authority and locked resting pose.
hypotheses.rationale: A recovery failure may be a morphology reachability limit rather than a controller gain problem.

hypotheses.description: Inspect joint-limit and self-contact frames, then change geometry, joint range, or recovery sequencing only inside an explicit complete-design lane.
hypotheses.rationale: The attempted recovery used structurally unsafe configurations.

hypotheses.description: Test delay-aware lateral-state feedback or foot-placement recovery without changing the fixed disturbance.
hypotheses.rationale: Measured lateral displacement exceeded the locked gate while the Controller owns the current recovery response.

hypotheses.description: Compare the commanded direction and speed with gait amplitude, phase, and planar feedback before changing the Task.
hypotheses.rationale: The measured planar velocity error exceeded the locked command-tracking gate.

hypotheses.description: Test a bounded left-right or front-rear steering differential against measured body yaw rate.
hypotheses.rationale: The measured yaw-rate error exceeded the locked command-tracking gate.

hypotheses.rationale: The case regressed beyond the locked baseline allowance.

stages.name: Command-conditioned locomotion
exitCriteria: All gating cases in the locked command-tracking Benchmark pass.
Every accepted Controller remains compatible with the declared Assembly interface.

stages.name: Autonomous self-righting
exitCriteria: All four locked resting-pose cases reach and hold the shared stable-standing target.
One deployed Controller autonomously hands authority from recovery back to locomotion and satisfies the locked post-recovery command.
Recovery respects joint-limit, collision, actuator, mass, cost, and Action-width gates.
The selected morphology retains required locked locomotion regressions.

stages.name: Continuous disturbance resilience
exitCriteria: Both locked continuous missions reach the stable recovery target after a real target loss.
The robot produces positive signed progress after the fixed recovery budget.
Atomic self-righting, handoff, command, and transition regressions remain passing.
A frozen learned Policy is promoted only if locked mission gates improve without regression.

stages.name: Transitions, braking, and visible motion quality
exitCriteria: All gating transition cases pass without regression against command-tracking.
A human-reviewed witness shows accepted motion without unresolved severe visual hypotheses.

stages.name: Robustness and sim-to-real evidence
exitCriteria: All gating held-out plant cases pass with immutable Policy and calibration provenance.
Shadow hardware capture preserves safety, state ABI, and Judge identities.

northStar.statement: Complete one continuous commanded mission through approach, bounded impact, autonomous recovery, and resumed locomotion without a simulation reset.

interventionSurfaces.rationale: The case regressed beyond the locked baseline allowance.
The attempted recovery used structurally unsafe configurations.
